conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie, I need to understand more about their circumstances.

How old are each of your children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 4 years old, Blake is 16, and Charlie is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand a bit more about each child's situation.

Are any of your children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie - currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex is only 4, so she's not in school yet - just nursery. Charlie is 15 and in school doing GCSEs. Blake is 16 and actually just left school recently - he's signed up with the careers service to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about where your children live.

Do all three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie - live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Charlie live with me, but Blake doesn't - he lives elsewhere.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I need to understand Blake's living situation a bit more.

Where does Blake live, and are you contributing to his upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake doesn't live with me anymore, but I do help support him financially. I contribute towards things like his food, clothes, and other expenses - I give about £50 a week for his upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

First, are you and your children living in the UK? And do you or your children have any specific circumstances such as:
- Any child currently in hospital or care?
- Are you or anyone else receiving child benefit for any of these children already?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, we live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. 

As for special circumstances, Alex has actually been in hospital for a few months now. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them - I haven't claimed it yet myself, that's why I'm checking if I'm eligible.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about Alex's hospital stay.

How long has Alex been in hospital, and is this expected to be a temporary stay or a longer-term situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been in hospital for about 12 weeks now. We're hoping it's temporary - the doctors are still treating her, but we don't know exactly when she'll be able to come home yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I have enough information now to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You appear to be eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children.**

Here's a summary for each child:

**Alex (age 4):** You can claim Child Benefit even though Alex is in hospital. The rules allow Child Benefit to continue for a child in hospital for up to 12 weeks initially, and this can be extended for up to 2 years in certain circumstances. Since Alex has been in hospital for about 12 weeks and this is expected to be temporary, you should be eligible. You'll need to inform Child Benefit about Alex's hospital stay.

**Blake (age 16):** Even though Blake doesn't live with you, you can still claim Child Benefit because you're contributing £50 per week towards his upkeep - which is more than the Child Benefit payment amount. Additionally, since Blake is registered with the careers service after leaving school, this may qualify as approved training.

**Charlie (age 15):** Charlie clearly meets all the eligibility requirements - living with you, under 16, and in full-time education doing GCSEs.

**Next steps:** You should apply for Child Benefit as soon as possible. When you apply, make sure to mention Alex's hospital stay and that you contribute to Blake's upkeep even though he doesn't live with you.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 4 years old (under 16) and lives with you in the UK. Although Alex has been in hospital for 12 weeks, according to the rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a child in hospital for up to 12 weeks (84 days) initially, and this can be extended for up to 2 years in certain circumstances. Since Alex has been in hospital for about 12 weeks and this is expected to be temporary with ongoing treatment, you should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. You would need to inform Child Benefit about the hospital stay.

Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you. However, under the rules, you can still claim Child Benefit for a child who doesn't live with you if you contribute at least the same amount (or more) towards their upkeep as the Child Benefit payment would be. You are contributing £50 per week towards Blake's upkeep for food, clothes, and expenses. The Child Benefit rate for a child (not the eldest) is significantly less than £50 per week, so you are contributing more than the Child Benefit amount. Additionally, Blake has recently left school at 16 and is signed up with the careers service. Child Benefit can continue until age 20 if a young person is in approved education or training. Being registered with the careers service may qualify as approved training/preparation. You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 15 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and is in full-time education doing GCSEs. Charlie clearly meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: You appear to be eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children. Alex (age 4) qualifies despite being in hospital as the stay is within the allowable period. Blake (age 16) qualifies because you contribute more than the Child Benefit amount towards his upkeep even though he doesn't live with you. Charlie (age 15) qualifies as they live with you and are in full-time education. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for these children, and you all live in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirements.
